model: sine-Gordon kink (continuum Lorentz-invariant; any pinning is a discretization artifact)
sine_gordon_weakly_pinned_note: SG barrier tiny even coarse; glide does not show coarse/fine contrast — PN-barrier scaling is the rigorous result
verdict: IS THE SPATIAL-LORENTZ FAILURE FUNDAMENTAL OR CURABLE? CURABLE — it is a coarse-discreteness artifact. PRIMARY RESULT (rigorous, direct): the Peierls-Nabarro barrier — the energy cost of sliding a static kink between lattice sites, i.e. the pinning potential ITSELF — collapses as the kink is resolved: 1.55e-03*E0 at a=1.0 (kink ~1.0 cell) -> 3.20e-05*E0 at a=0.7 -> BELOW NUMERICAL PRECISION (0) by a=0.5 (~2.0 cells) — the textbook ~exp(-const/a) collapse (barrier_shrinks=True). Once a pattern is resolved over even ~2 cells, the lattice presents NO preferred position -> no pinning force -> the pattern can sit/move at any sub-cell location with equal energy -> the spatial frame is HIDDEN. So Phase-5's spatial-Lorentz failure is NOT fundamental to discreteness; it is the signature of an UNDER-RESOLVED pattern (Phase-5's soliton spanned ~few cells with a strong-barrier nonlinearity). THE PHYSICAL-SCALE POINT (the real resolution): the barrier scales ~exp(-const*N), N = cells-per-pattern (data: ~48x drop from 1->1.4 cells => const~9/cell). A REAL particle on a REAL Planck grid spans N ~ Compton/Planck ~ 10^20 cells => PN barrier ~ exp(-10^21) = absolute zero. So Phase-5's pinning was a NUMERICAL under-resolution artifact (few-cell solitons, for tractability); at the physical scale the spatial preferred frame is hidden to exp(-10^20) -> the make-or-break RESOLVES in the model's favor at the physical scale. SECONDARY (dynamical glide, honest): a boosted kink glides with ~100% velocity retention at BOTH a=1.0 and a=0.25 (both_glide=True) — but this does NOT reproduce-then-cure Phase-5 pinning, because SINE-GORDON is only WEAKLY pinned (barrier 1.6e-03*E0 even coarse, far below a v=0.3 kink's kinetic energy), so it glides at any spacing. Reproducing STRONG dynamical pinning needs a larger-barrier system (phi^4) or a slow kink; the PN-barrier-vs-resolution SCALING above is the universal, rigorous answer regardless of system. CONCLUSION: this UPGRADES Phase-5's partial-negative — the make-or-break spatial hurdle is REDUCIBLE (a resolution / discretization issue, not a fundamental wall). THE DEEPER OPEN QUESTION (P1-deep, named not built): a genuinely-discrete Planck substrate cannot 'refine'; the meaningful test is whether a TRANSLATIONALLY-INVARIANT discretization (Speight-Ward / integrable-lattice) has ZERO PN barrier at FIXED COARSE a. Those schemes exist; testing one is the next step. HONEST: PN-barrier-vanishing-with-resolution is textbook; the content is that it locates Phase-5's obstruction as non-fundamental and points the substrate at TI discretizations. Bucket 0 unchanged.
